reviews.review_msg: hw was easy to do well in. tests were hard bc the answers were not always in slides, they need u to be very specific in ur answers. but i liked that the final was not cummulative. i spent around

Her passion for science is visible in the course. The lectures are nicely structured she would explain concepts and provide examples.The slides are on the emptier side but all her lectures are recorded and the main points are written down. She is the sweetest person in office hours, she does not hesitate to reexplain concept to you. ily

Dr. Palen's lectures are well presented, and the content is intriguing. Her exams can be on the difficult side, but there are numerous projects to help your mark. Some people here say her grading scale is weird, but for Fall 2024, she used a standard SFU scale. Just prep well for exams and ask questions in lecture/tutorial.

I received an A but I'd say do not take Ecology with her. Her slides ARE USELESS. She has a lot of experience in Ecology, but she wants you to apply super specific and very comprehensive material in the exams which some of them are not covered in the lecture and it is supposed to be covered during the tutorials. Her slides are just bunch of photos

Wendy is one of the best professors in biology you can bump into. She is so nice, enthusiastic about the materials, and really cares about you. She also discusses how profound climate change is to life and really appreciates enthusiasm to the course. However, her exams have so much concept applications and memorization stuff.

Her slides are disorganised and not helpful when you want to review course materials to study for exams. The formatting of her exams are also unclear and messy, oftentimes you are running out of space to write. The notes she provided for revision have photos overlapping the actual text so they are useless which is unfortunate.

Wendy is a very caring professor who is accessible outside of class, but her grading criteria for this course is beyond confusing to say the least. It doesn't help that the TAs and instructors have different marking criteria and gives out contradicting instructions when you ask for them.

Dr. Palen is great at explaining concepts and her enthusiasm for the course material is contagious. However, the grading rubrics for assignments were extremely vague, and this combined with petty marking and TA's who give conflicting advice on papers and assignments made for a very frustrating experience.

really nice prof, gives time to her students and is available for help. class attendance is mandatory most of the questions form her exam are from lectures. No book required. Weekly assignments due worth 5% each, doing them is important for doing well in the class.

Wendy was awesome! She made the course interesting, engaging, and relevant to your everyday life. Honestly, a wonderful class and I'd love to take more classes with her if she taught others. The TAs I had this semester were pretty tough markers, and the class grading scale isn't the norm so it's not a super easy A but can be a GPA booster
